hook: ChatGPT keeps explaining things to you like you've never used a computer before.
caption:
ChatGPT keeps explaining things to you like you've never used a computer before.

You ask for help structuring a business proposal and it starts with: "A business proposal is a document that..."

Bro. I KNOW what a proposal is. I just need help with the flow.

This is the thing nobody talks about with AI frustration. It's not that the AI is wrong. It's that it doesn't know WHERE you are.

It doesn't know you've been in your industry for 11 years. It doesn't know you think in frameworks, not bullet points. It doesn't know you hate over-explained context and just want the answer.

So it defaults to: explain everything, assume nothing, offend no one.

Which ends up being useful to nobody.

BootFile fixes this by building a profile of how YOU actually think — your background, your communication style, your tolerance for hand-holding (spoiler: zero).

Then every AI interaction starts from THAT baseline instead of "assume user is a golden retriever who just discovered the internet."

Your AI should already know you're smart.

It just needs someone to tell it that.
